# State Management Checklist: Product-Line Switcher and activeWorkspace Scoping

**Purpose**: Validate Facility/Product Line state, persistence, hydration, invalidation, and cross-tab synchronization requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-04-26
**Feature**: [Link to spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are Product Line scope and `activeTenant` defined as independent state concepts? [Completeness, Spec FR-005, FR-013]
- [x] CHK002 Is Facility scope defined as discriminated runtime state, with `activeWorkspace = null` limited to compatibility storage after initialization? [Clarity, Spec FR-013, FR-014]
- [x] CHK003 Is `setActiveProductLine(productLine | null, options)` specified as the only public transition path for user selection, hydration, URL adoption, invalid reset, and cross-tab acceptance? [Completeness, Spec FR-006]
- [x] CHK004 Is the Zustand persistence key and persisted slice boundary specified so only Product Line scope state is stored under `mc:active-workspace:v1`? [Completeness, Spec FR-007, Plan Technical Context]
- [x] CHK005 Are `scopeKey` ownership requirements specified for mode-sensitive requests, caches, URL state, and state survival? [Completeness, Spec FR-009, Key Entities]

## Hydration and Persistence

- [x] CHK006 Are stale or unauthorized persisted Product Line ids required to be validated after `/api/workspaces` before scoped data renders? [Completeness, Spec FR-007, SC-006]
- [x] CHK007 Are malformed localStorage payloads, wrong-version persisted values, and persisted real `facility` row ids specified as rejected hydration inputs? [Edge Cases, Spec FR-007]
- [x] CHK008 Are `activeTenant` changes specified to clear Product Line scope and ignore persisted values from the previous tenant before new scoped data renders? [Edge Cases, Spec FR-007]

## Cross-Tab Synchronization

- [x] CHK009 Are BroadcastChannel messages required to include tenant and user/session guards? [Completeness, Spec FR-008]
- [x] CHK010 Is stale BroadcastChannel version rejection specified in requirements and design artifacts? [Consistency, Spec FR-008, Research Decision 4]
- [x] CHK011 Is the BroadcastChannel unavailable fallback specified consistently between no-op behavior and reload/manual convergence behavior? [Consistency, Spec Edge Cases, SC-003]
- [x] CHK012 Is cross-tab convergence quantified with a 1 second threshold when BroadcastChannel is available? [Measurability, Spec SC-003]

## Scope Change Invalidation

- [x] CHK013 Are incompatible `activeProject`, selected task, selected agent, selected project, selected conversation, modal, filter, and draft states required to clear unless keyed by `scopeKey`? [Completeness, Spec FR-009]
- [x] CHK014 Are stale in-flight responses and optimistic mutation completions required to be ignored when their captured `scopeKey` no longer matches the active scope? [Completeness, Data Model Scope Key, Spec SC-014]
- [x] CHK015 Are local filters and drafts included in the invalidation requirements rather than only high-level selected entities? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]

## Consistency and Scope Boundaries

- [x] CHK016 Are real `workspaces.slug='facility'` row ids consistently rejected as Product Line scope in REST, URL, SSE, and switcher semantics? [Consistency, Spec FR-004, FR-015]
- [x] CHK017 Does the implementation plan avoid contradicting the required store/API/SSE state work with an overly narrow production-code scope statement? [Consistency, Plan Technical Context]
- [x] CHK018 Are skills and local/gateway sessions/transcripts explicitly excluded from Product Line-owned state for this phase? [Consistency, Spec FR-012]

## Remediation Re-run

- [x] CHK019 Are all state-management gap markers from the initial run resolved in generated SPEC-002 artifacts? [Completeness, Spec FR-007, FR-008, SC-014]
- [x] CHK020 Does the rerun preserve the Product Line scope boundary without adding production runtime code? [Scope Control, Plan Strict Scope]
